evidence_quotes.quote: It's main goal is to protect consumer video games rights and forcing developers give games an end of life plan.

Companies sometimes just end support for a game and leave you no way to ever play it again because they have made a game that could be played completely offline an online service that cannot run without their support.
I once bought minecraft when mojang owned the IP.
Later Microsoft bought it and removed my access to the game whilst I paid for it in the past.
I contacted their support and they refuse to give me access back to the game I have bought and owned in the past.
Video game consumer rights need to improve and stop developers to simply take access away when they feel like it.
Jagex the developers of oldschool RuneScape have banned an account of mine because I was in a clan that was deemed controversial whilst not breaking any ingame or real life laws.
Just being part of this clan made them remove my access to the game because they felt like it.
Again contacting costumer support leads to nothing.
Please help improve consumer rights for video games.

paragraphs.text: Please also include videogames into this law. There is a petition going on called stop killing games. It's main goal is to protect consumer video games rights and forcing developers give games an end of life plan. Companies sometimes just end support for a game and leave you no way to ever play it again because they have made a game that could be played completely offline an online service that cannot run without their support. I once bought minecraft when mojang owned the IP. Later Microsoft bought it and removed my access to the game whilst I paid for it in the past. I contacted their support and they refuse to give me access back to the game I have bought and owned in the past. Video game consumer rights need to improve and stop developers to simply take access away when they feel like it. Jagex the developers of oldschool RuneScape have banned an account of mine because I was in a clan that was deemed controversial whilst not breaking any ingame or real life laws. Just being part of this clan made them remove my access to the game because they felt like it. Again contacting costumer support leads to nothing. I get completely ignored. Please help improve consumer rights for video games.
